hero.intro: I explore how infrastructure connects the physical world to software: hardware, RF, rail systems, and the digital layers that cities run on. I study electrical engineering at Bath and work in Engineering & Technology at Transport for London, with a tendency to over-engineer everything in between.

projects.page_subtitle: a catalogue of things i've built

descriptions.goldeneye-uav: VTOL surveillance platform with custom RF and propulsion design
descriptions.platenations: Licence plate OCR sighting tracker with map visualisation
descriptions.rail-labels: Asset tagging system for UK rail infrastructure
descriptions.rf-sounder: Sub-GHz channel sounding rig built on SDR hardware
descriptions.transit-dash: Live realtime board for local rail and bus arrivals

experience.page_subtitle: work and education, in rough order.

about.bio: [PLACEHOLDER] I'm an engineer working across rail systems, RF, and applied hardware. I like building things that connect physical infrastructure to legible software — typically self-hosted, usually over-engineered.

contact.subtitle: best reached by email. happy to chat about rail, RF, or anything connected.
